chapters.title: LiteLLM Core: Unifying LLM Access
chapters.goal: Understand LiteLLM's fundamental purpose of providing a unified API for diverse LLMs and make initial API calls.
key_concepts: Choosing between SDK and Proxy Server

chapters.title: Connecting to Diverse LLM Providers and Basic Interaction
chapters.goal: Learn how to configure API keys for various LLM providers and make basic synchronous and streaming requests using LiteLLM.
key_concepts: Setting environment variables for different providers (OpenAI, Anthropic, VertexAI, NVIDIA, HuggingFace, Azure, Ollama, Openrouter, Novita AI)

chapters.title: Deep Dive into Completion Function Parameters and Error Handling
chapters.goal: Understand the detailed input and output structure of LiteLLM's `completion()` function, including required/optional parameters, and implement robust error management.
key_concepts: Unified exception handling across LLM providers

chapters.title: Exploring Supported AI Models and Embeddings
chapters.goal: Identify and utilize the wide array of AI completion and embedding models available through LiteLLM across various providers.
key_concepts: Overview of supported chat completion models (OpenAI, Azure, Cohere, Anthropic)
OpenRouter and Novita AI model support

chapters.title: Basic Observability with LiteLLM Callbacks
chapters.goal: Implement basic logging and data output for LLM calls using LiteLLM's callback system to send data to general services.
key_concepts: Sending output data to Posthog, Sentry, and Lunary
Setting environment variables for logging tools
Tracking costs, usage, and latency with callbacks

chapters.title: Advanced Observability Integrations
chapters.goal: Integrate LiteLLM with specific observability platforms like Helicone and Supabase for detailed logging, cost tracking, and usage analysis.
key_concepts: Helicone integration for logging across all LLM providers
Using Helicone as a proxy for OpenAI/Azure requests
Supabase integration for request logging and total spend tracking
Creating and configuring Supabase tables for logs
Additional controls for Supabase integration (table names, end-user identification)

chapters.title: LiteLLM Proxy Server: Deployment and Core Concepts
chapters.goal: Understand the architecture and core benefits of the LiteLLM Proxy Server as an LLM gateway and deploy it using CLI or Docker.
key_concepts: Introduction to the LiteLLM Proxy Server (LLM Gateway)
Benefits of using the proxy (unified interface, load balancing, cost tracking, guardrails)
Making chat completion requests to the proxy

chapters.title: Advanced Proxy Configuration and Management
chapters.goal: Configure the LiteLLM Proxy using `config.yaml` for advanced model management, virtual keys, and comprehensive spend tracking.
key_concepts: Creating and managing `config.yaml` for model definitions
Proxy virtual keys and spend management
Rate limiting and budget enforcement through the proxy

reasoning: The curriculum is designed to progressively introduce LiteLLM, starting with its core value proposition and basic Python SDK usage. It then moves to advanced SDK features like streaming and detailed parameter control, followed by a comprehensive overview of supported models. Observability is introduced next, beginning with general callbacks and advancing to specific integrations. Finally, the curriculum covers the LiteLLM Proxy Server, starting with deployment and core concepts, and concluding with advanced configuration and management features. This structure ensures a foundational understanding before delving into more complex topics, providing a senior software engineer with a logical path to master LiteLLM for both individual projects and enterprise-level LLM management.
